subject: Re: TeamTomo and latest Warp/M versions?
body:
Hi Matt,

I don’t think there is a newer build available - sorry about the problems with the GoldNet. Do you get the same errors with the pretrained model included in your installation?

Cheers,

Alister

> On 4 Jan 2022, at 19:06, Matt Larson <larsonmattr@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> 
> We are trying to complete the https://teamtomo.org tutorial for EMPIAR-10164 (HIV VLPs) which combines Warp-RELION-M and Dynamo.
> 
>  Our environment is a Windows 10 PC with a single NVIDIA 1080 GPU. We are running the Warp 1.0.9 version, with the 'copy and replace' of files using the Warp nightly build 2020-11-04.  I believe this is the latest available release on the website of http://www.warpem.com/warp/?page_id=65 
> 
>  Prior to using the 2020-11-04 nightly build, we had crashes with the GUI during initial preprocessing. The use of the nightly build code appears to have resolved these.
> 
>  Now we are hitting an error when using the TeamTomo GoldNet model, where a TensorFlow.TFException: Attempting to use uninitialized value.conv2d_25/kernel, causes the step (https://teamtomo.org/walkthroughs/EMPIAR-10164/2d-preprocessing.html) to fail when using Pick Particles with the GoldNet model.  This step is to mask out beads prior to tomographic reconstruction.
> 
>  Before we go further to diagnose and troubleshoot Warp/M, is there a newer total build beyond the 2020-11-04?  We would like to work with the latest stable version of Warp and ideally the same version as TeamTomo has used to test the workflows.
> 
>  If anyone can point out where we can get the latest build for Warp with or without an installer, please let me know!
> 
>  Thanks,
>   Matt
> 
>  ------------------------
> Matt Larson
> 
> Associate Scientist
> 
> Computer Scientist/System Administrator
> 
> Cryo-EM Research Center
> 
> University of Wisconsin Madison
> 
> 440 Henry Mall. Room 1220
> 
> Madison, WI 53706
